prompt:
Ticket: habit tracker CLI for the team wellness pilot. Stdlib only, python 3.12. I want three modules at minimum -- `cli.py` (argument parsing / commands), `habits.py` (streak math, pure functions, no file IO), `store.py` (JSON persistence) -- split further if you like, but those three names are load-bearing because the mobile team will import `habits` directly.

CLI (every command takes `--db PATH`, a JSON file your store module owns the format of):

    python3 cli.py --db h.json add water --schedule daily
    python3 cli.py --db h.json add review --schedule weekly
    python3 cli.py --db h.json done water --date 2026-03-10
    python3 cli.py --db h.json report --today 2026-03-11

- `add NAME --schedule daily|weekly`: registers a habit. Adding a name that already exists must fail: nonzero exit, error on stderr mentioning the name, db unchanged. Any schedule other than daily/weekly: nonzero exit.
- `done NAME --date YYYY-MM-DD`: records a completion. There is deliberately no 'today' default -- the date is always explicit so runs are reproducible. Marking the same habit done twice on the same date is fine but must not double-count anything. Unknown habit name: nonzero exit, stderr mentions the name. Malformed date: nonzero exit.
- `report --today YYYY-MM-DD`: one line per habit, sorted by habit name, tab-separated, exactly: `NAME\tSCHEDULE\tstreak=N\tbest=M\tSTATUS` where STATUS is `done` if the habit is already satisfied for the current period (that calendar day for daily; the current ISO week for weekly) and `due` otherwise. No habits yet (including the db file not existing at all): print nothing, exit 0.

Streak semantics (this is the part people get wrong, so nailing it in `habits.py` is the actual ticket):
- `habits.current_streak(dates, schedule, today)` -> int. `dates` is any iterable of 'YYYY-MM-DD' strings, unordered, possibly with duplicates. Daily: count consecutive calendar days ending at `today` if today is done, otherwise ending at yesterday if yesterday is done, otherwise 0 (an in-progress streak survives until you've missed a full period). Weekly: same idea over ISO calendar weeks -- a week counts if it has at least one completion; the streak ends at the current ISO week if satisfied, else the previous ISO week, else 0. ISO weeks, not 'last 7 days' -- year boundaries must work (2025-12-28 is 2025-W52, 2025-12-29 is already 2026-W01).
- `habits.best_streak(dates, schedule)` -> int, the longest consecutive run anywhere in history, same period rules.

Every command runs as its own process, so state must round-trip through the db file between invocations. `python3 test_habits.py` is the acceptance gate; don't touch it.
